content:
Matt Pocock’s four pillars, Anthropic’s own checklist, and the maintenance protocol neither manual wrote.

10 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Photo by James Wainscoat on UnsplashRead this for free
I collect Claude skills the way people collect gym memberships. A repo crosses 200,000 stars, my feed lights up, and ten seconds later it’s in my folder. I’m at 21 skills now, plus everything the plugins dragged in.
Somewhere past skill fifteen, I lost track of which ones still fired.
I suppose I’m not alone…
One developer measured where that road ends. His setup: 28 custom skills plus a stack from plugins, eight MCP servers, 15 rule files. He wrote ten test prompts, each crafted to trigger a specific skill, and ran them. Zero fired. Anthropic closed his bug report as “not planned.”
Another skill author summed up the same experience in six words: “No error. No warning. No log.”
Skills fail in three ways : They stop firing. They vanish from Claude’s menu, or they swell with edits until they steer the model wrong.
The manuals teach you to write skills.
This piece is about the missing half: why skills rot, the checklist that repairs them, and the…
